claim: Ultra Code (dynamic workflows) allows Claude to design its own workflow and spin up sub-agents with clean context for individual tasks instead of attempting everything in a single context window
actionable: Use /ultracode command to enable dynamic workflows for complex tasks requiring multiple agents; this prevents context degradation and stops premature task completion
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Auto mode uses a classifier to check each action and only asks for approval on genuinely risky decisions, unlike dangerously-skip-permissions which removes all safety checks
actionable: Enable auto mode (shift tab twice) to approve actions automatically for non-risky operations while maintaining security for destructive actions
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Use /loop with /goal together to create autonomous long-running tasks that repeat on a cadence and don't stop until a specified condition is met
actionable: Use /loop [interval] and /goal [condition] paired to create recurring tasks that automatically repeat until outcome conditions are achieved, e.g., loop every day + goal 'inbox empty'
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Well-built skills should be under 200 lines with progressive disclosure of context, separate reference materials in folders, and a self-learning mechanism that captures feedback in a rules section
actionable: Structure skills as <200 line instruction guides with external reference context in separate folders; add a rules section that accumulates and reads feedback from previous runs
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Skills should be built as reusable Lego blocks that work across multiple skill systems rather than hyper-specific to one task
actionable: Design each skill (e.g., copywriting, visual identity) to be composable and pluggable across multiple workflows; avoid coupling to a single use case
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers load tool definitions into context permanently and consume thousands of tokens before use, whereas CLI tools are only called when needed
actionable: Use MCP for day-to-day interactive tools (CRM, database) where rich interactions justify token cost; use CLI for simple, occasional, predictable actions (messaging, file fetching)
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Use semantic search (frameworks like memarch or Hermes) for Claude memory instead of keyword search to enable meaningful recall of past context across sessions
actionable: Implement semantic memory search using open-source frameworks (memarch, Hermes) with vector database storage to enable recall by meaning rather than keywords
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Define folder structures in claude.md to separate brand context, client context, and workstation context so Claude loads the right context at the right time
actionable: Create a folder structure (brand/, clients/, departments/) and document in claude.md how to reference these folders during specific tasks
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Save plans to files in project folders rather than the default global disposable folder so they survive context compaction through long sessions
actionable: When planning longer tasks (>1 hour), save the plan as a file inside project folders so Claude can re-read it as context is compacted
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Use /rewind to roll back to earlier checkpoints instead of correcting bad outputs; adding corrections adds broken code back to context, degrading quality further
actionable: When Claude outputs incorrect code, use /rewind to return to the last good state, then re-prompt with additional context rather than asking for fixes
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Use agent view (type /agents) to manage multiple parallel tasks by grouping conversations by repository and status, enabling goal-driven work rather than supervising individual sessions
actionable: Launch /agents to see dashboard view of all active agents grouped by repository and status; reply to agents needing input from overview page
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Make your setup portable by using agents.md (open standard), skills (open standard), and CLI/MCP tools supported by all major tools so you can migrate off Claude Code if needed
actionable: Structure your setup using agents.md, skills.md, and standard tool connections so you can migrate to Cursor, Copilot, or other tools without lock-in
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Run Claude Code 24/7 on a VPS with persistent sessions (using Tmux or similar) and Telegram channels to dispatch tasks from anywhere without needing your laptop
actionable: Set up Claude Code on a VPS, use Tmux for persistent sessions, and connect Telegram for remote task dispatch and approval without laptop access
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS

claim: Skills are what Claude knows how to do, while sub-agents are who does the work; use skills for context you need back in the main session, sub-agents for isolated context that would flood the main session
actionable: Use skills when you need intermediate outputs in the main context; use sub-agents when you need context isolation, different tools, or parallel execution
source_title: 14 GENIUS Ways to Give Claude Code SUPERPOWERS
